county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Hood County formed 1866 from Johnson. County seat is Granbury. North Texas / Blackland Prairie region with farming and urban growth heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Granbury. North-central Texas county. Hood County Museum. Granbury Town Square historic district. Granbury Opera House. Check Johnson County for pre-1866 records. Comanche Peak nuclear plant records (20th century).
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Hood County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1866; County Clerk in Granbury holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Hood County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1866 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Hood County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1866. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: Hood County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1866. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Hood County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1866 to 1950 for Hood County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Native American Records -- Hood County Area (various tribes)
source_profiles.repository: NARA, Fort Worth / Oklahoma Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Pre-settlement and frontier-era records for various tribes in the Hood County area. Includes Indian Affairs records, military campaign records, and reservation records at NARA Fort Worth. Texas frontier counties experienced significant Native American conflict through the 1870s.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Hood County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Hood County from 1866. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Hopkins County formed 1846 from Lamar, Nacogdoches. County seat is Sulphur Springs. North Texas / Blackland Prairie region with farming and urban growth heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Sulphur Springs. Northeast Texas county. Hopkins County Historical Society. Southwest Dairy Museum. Check Lamar and Nacogdoches for pre-1846 records. Agricultural and dairy heritage.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Hopkins County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1846; County Clerk in Sulphur Springs holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Hopkins County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1846 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Hopkins County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1846. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Hopkins County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for Hopkins County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Hopkins County Enslaved Persons Records -- TSLAC / County Clerk
source_profiles.repository: TSLAC, Austin / County Clerk, Sulphur Springs
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Estate inventories listing enslaved persons, bills of sale, tax rolls listing slaveholders and enslaved persons counts, and Freedmen's Bureau records (NARA). Hopkins County was part of the Texas plantation economy.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Hopkins County from 1846. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Houston County formed 1837 from Nacogdoches. County seat is Crockett. East Texas / Piney Woods region with timber industry and plantation heritage. BURNED COUNTY: 1865 courthouse fire; many records destroyed.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Crockett. 1865 courthouse fire destroyed many records — check TSLAC for backups. East Texas county — one of the original Texas counties. Houston County Historical Commission. Davy Crockett heritage. Mission Tejas State Park. Caddo Indian heritage. Check Nacogdoches for pre-1837 records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Houston County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1837; County Clerk in Crockett holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Houston County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1865 to present. Some gaps due to 1865 courthouse fire; many records destroyed.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1865. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1865. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for Houston County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Estate inventories listing enslaved persons, bills of sale, tax rolls listing slaveholders and enslaved persons counts, and Freedmen's Bureau records (NARA). Houston County was part of the Texas plantation economy.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Houston County Land Grants
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Republic of Texas and early statehood land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Houston County. GLO archives are online with searchable databases. Texas managed its own public lands (unique among states). Empresario colony records if applicable.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Hudspeth County formed 1917 from El Paso. County seat is Sierra Blanca. West Texas / Permian Basin region with ranching, oil, and frontier heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Sierra Blanca. Trans-Pecos county created 1917 from El Paso. Hudspeth County Historical Society. Salt War of San Elizario (1877) heritage. Check El Paso for pre-1917 records. Southern Pacific Railroad records. Very sparse population.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Hunt County formed 1846 from Fannin, Nacogdoches. County seat is Greenville. North Texas / Blackland Prairie region with farming and urban growth heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Greenville. North Texas county. Hunt County Historical Commission. Audie Murphy (most decorated WWII soldier) born in Hunt County. Check Fannin and Nacogdoches for pre-1846 records. Cotton farming heritage. Significant African American community.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Hutchinson County formed 1876 from Bexar. County seat is Stinnett. Texas Panhandle / South Plains region with ranching and farming heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Stinnett. Panhandle county organized 1901. Hutchinson County Museum in Borger. Oil boom (Borger oil boom, 1926). Adobe Walls battle sites (1864, 1874). Check Bexar for pre-organization records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Jackson County formed 1836 from Original Texas county (Municipality of Jackson). County seat is Edna. Gulf Coast region with port, plantation, and petrochemical heritage. One of the original Texas counties from the Republic of Texas era.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Edna. Original Texas county (Gulf Coast). Jackson County Historical Commission. Texana Museum in Edna. Ranching and farming. Check for early Republic-era records at TSLAC. Czech immigrant community.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Jackson County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1836; County Clerk in Edna holds deed books
